judge.rationale: This implementation is severe architectural slop: it does not implement merge_policy at all. Instead it imports a Dafny-compiled module and attempts to alias symbols from it, but never defines the required entry point function. The code will almost certainly fail at import time (no 'module_' exists), and even if it didn't, there is no merge_policy function anywhere. The approach is maximally indirect, opaque, and non-functional—the antithesis of the Zen of Python's values of simplicity, explicitness, and readability.
